summary: iOS | Morpheus | Unable to track inCall status
scenario: Morpheus devices do not report call state via deckardId 0x0E35, preventing in-call alerts, DFU blocking, and ANC transparency mode switching; fix uses deckardId 0x0E22 to correctly reflect call state.
description_clean: The iOS application cannot reliably determine if a user is in a call when connected to Morpheus devices. This prevents the app from showing appropriate alerts or blocking DFU initiation and ANC transparency mode switching during an active call. The root cause is that Morpheus devices do not send or respond to deckardId 0x0E35. The fix is to additionally use deckardId 0x0E22, which correctly reflects call state on Morpheus devices. Acceptance criteria: all devices correctly respond to in-call status; DFU availability is enabled/blocked based on call state; ANC transparency control is enabled/disabled accordingly.
steps: 1. Connect iOS application to a Morpheus device.
2. Initiate an active call on the iOS device.
3. Observe whether the app detects the in-call status using deckardId 0x0E35.
4. Note that Morpheus devices do not send or respond to deckardId 0x0E35, causing the app to fail to detect the call.
5. Implement the fix by additionally using deckardId 0x0E22 to check call state on Morpheus devices.
6. Verify that all devices correctly respond to in-call status via deckardId 0x0E22.
7. Confirm that DFU availability is enabled when not in a call and blocked during an active call.
8. Confirm that ANC transparency mode switching is enabled when not in a call and disabled during an active call.
expected: The iOS application correctly detects when a user is in an active call on Morpheus devices. DFU initiation is blocked and ANC transparency mode switching is disabled during an active call. Both are re-enabled when the call ends.
actual: The iOS application cannot reliably determine if a user is in a call when connected to Morpheus devices. Morpheus devices do not send or respond to deckardId 0x0E35, so the app fails to show appropriate alerts or block DFU initiation and ANC transparency mode switching during an active call.
